I couldn't find any information on the plant "Nestylix arborea." However, I can provide you with a paragraph on a different plant, "Silybum marianum," commonly known as Milk Thistle, which is a flowering plant with the species name "marianum" and is known as "Nestlix marianum" in some literature.

Milk Thistle (Silybum marianum) is a flowering plant of the daisy and sunflower family, known for its unique purple flowers and edible seeds. The health benefits of Milk Thistle include liver protection, antioxidant properties, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a popular supplement for liver health and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Milk Thistle include silymarin, a flavonoid complex, and silibinin, a potent antioxidant, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Milk Thistle include capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as extracts and tinctures used in traditional medicine and modern supplements.
